title: Happy Anniversary

jokes.text: What would you like us to say? / Well, you could start by saying, 'Happy anniversary.'
jokes.setup: Father asks kids to wish mother happy anniversary
jokes.punchline: Kids treat it like they need explicit instructions for basic courtesy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing the Rose kids' privileged detachment from normal family dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Kids, I want you to wish your mother a happy anniversary.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Happy anniversary, Mom.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, happy anniversary.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: With a little more enthusiasm, perhaps?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: HAPPY ANNIVERSARY, MOM!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Happy anniversary! We're so glad you married Dad!

jokes.text: Isn't that something that you two are supposed to say to each other?
jokes.setup: Kids being asked to wish parents happy anniversary
jokes.punchline: Logical but emotionally tone-deaf response about anniversary protocol
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Alexis's shallow understanding of family bonds

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, you guys are celebrating your anniversary? That's so sweet. How many years has it been?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Twenty-seven years.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's amazing. So you guys are basically due for a divorce then, right?

jokes.text: If we're truth telling, the Bloomfields were always a little too friendly with one another.
jokes.setup: Mention of the Bloomfield family throwing anniversary parties
jokes.punchline: Implication of inappropriate family closeness
jokes.explanation: Smart setup for the punchline that follows, building tension

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: The Bloomfields throw anniversary parties every year. It's their thing.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: How... quaint. Though I suppose when you've been together that long, you run out of conversation topics and resort to celebrating the fact that you haven't murdered each other.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, they seem happy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, Dad. That's what happens when you marry someone who isn't your cousin.

jokes.text: I just don't understand what kind of family skinny dips together.
jokes.setup: Previous line about Bloomfields being 'too friendly'
jokes.punchline: Specific uncomfortable detail that explains the 'too friendly' comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup, specific and uncomfortable in the right way

jokes.text: Congratulations on your ongoing love for one another. You did it!
jokes.setup: Kids asked to say happy anniversary
jokes.punchline: Treating love like an achievement to be congratulated
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the kids' detached, performative approach to family emotion

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, that's depressing.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? They're being sweet.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: They're treating love like an achievement to be congratulated. Next, they'll be asking us to show them our participation trophies.

jokes.text: You sound like the incestuous Bloomfields.
jokes.setup: Kids' awkward anniversary wishes
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to the previously mentioned inappropriate family
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that ties the scene together but not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, at least they're not as inappropriate as your mother.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Excuse me?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, the kids were sweet. They tried.

jokes.text: You wanna guess what your present is? / No, I do not. / Dinner!
jokes.setup: Roland offering a present to the Roses
jokes.punchline: Johnny's refusal followed by Roland telling him anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Roland's obliviousness to social cues

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I got something for you.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh, that's very kind of you, Roland, but that's really not necessary.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm telling you anyway.

jokes.text: That sounds too good to... what's the catch?
jokes.setup: Roland offering free dinner at exclusive restaurant
jokes.punchline: Johnny's suspicious mid-sentence realization
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Johnny's learned wariness of Roland's offers

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I got us a table at that fancy restaurant you've been wanting to go to. My treat.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Really? That's very generous of you, Roland. What's the—wait, is this because you need something from me?

jokes.text: Yeah, we have a coupon. Four entrees for the price of three.
jokes.setup: Offering dinner at 'exclusive' restaurant
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just a coupon deal, deflating the exclusivity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland moment - well-meaning but completely missing the point of elegance

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I've arranged a very exclusive dinner for you at the finest restaurant in town.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I pulled some strings, called in a favor. We're talking VIP treatment.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I got a two-for-one coupon.

jokes.text: Boy, that was a close call.
jokes.setup: Roland and Jocelyn leaving after the coupon revelation
jokes.punchline: Johnny's relief at avoiding the awkward dinner situation
jokes.explanation: Functional but predictable reaction beat, gets the job done

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Thank God. I was dreading that dinner.

jokes.text: Stevie Budd was voted: 'Most likely to get the guy.' With that hair cut?!
jokes.setup: Looking at Stevie's yearbook
jokes.punchline: Incredulity at her high school popularity despite her appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's superficial judgments

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You were popular in high school?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was actually pretty popular.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: But you look like that.

jokes.text: And girls, it seems.
jokes.setup: Stevie saying she was popular with boys
jokes.punchline: David noticing something in the yearbook suggesting she was also popular with girls
jokes.explanation: Economical line that suggests Stevie's broad appeal, delivered with perfect David snark

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I'm looking at your yearbook photo, and I can see why the boys were interested. Though I have to say, based on the number of signatures from girls saying 'You were so fun!' and 'Best summer ever!' with little hearts, I'm sensing your appeal may have been somewhat broader.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: What? That's just how people wrote in yearbooks.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm. And yet, I'm not seeing this level of enthusiasm in the boys' section.

jokes.text: What series? / Dateline. / I played a boy who got abducted from a grocery store.
jokes.setup: David bragging about his headshot getting him TV work
jokes.punchline: It was a crime reenactment show, not actual acting
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that deflates David's ego perfectly, with great specificity

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, my headshot got me a television role.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Really? That's great.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, it was a crime reenactment show.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh. So not actual acting then.

jokes.text: People thought I actually was the 'Value-mart victim.'
jokes.setup: David explaining his Dateline appearance
jokes.punchline: His performance was so convincing people thought he was the real victim
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows David's pride even in this absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: My performance was so convincing people thought I was the real victim.

jokes.text: Hmm, bet that got you laid. / It did. It really did.
jokes.setup: David's convincing performance as abduction victim
jokes.punchline: David admitting he leveraged fake victimhood for sex
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly in character for both - Stevie's blunt observation, David's shameless honesty

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That was a really convincing performance.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Thank you. I was actually abducted by aliens.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No, you weren't.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, I wasn't. But it was a really effective way to have sex with people.

jokes.text: Are you telepathic or something, because I have been thinking about asking you that question every day this week.
jokes.setup: David jokingly asking if she wants him to leave early
jokes.punchline: Stevie's brutally honest response about actually wanting him gone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie moment - deadpan delivery of something genuinely harsh but funny

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No, stay. No, actually, yes. Leave now.

jokes.text: I was just kidding. / Oh... no yeah, me too.
jokes.setup: Stevie's harsh but honest response
jokes.punchline: David's awkward backpedaling when he realizes she was serious
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the uncomfortable dynamic between them

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know, Stevie, I've been thinking about what you said, and I really don't think it was that bad.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, I'm serious. I mean, yes, perhaps my tone was a bit off, but the content was—

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You were terrible.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh. You're... you're serious right now?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Very.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I appreciate your honesty, and I'm glad we could have this conversation, and I will certainly take what you've said into consideration moving forward.

jokes.text: Mutt's having a soiree? / Well, I'm sure you're invited. / Yeah, no. Yeah, no, why wouldn't I be?
jokes.setup: David learning about Mutt's party
jokes.punchline: His obvious insecurity disguised as casual confidence
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing David's fragile ego around his ex

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So Mutt's throwing a party? That's... nice. I mean, I wasn't invited, obviously, because I have my own things going on. Very important things. But good for him. Parties are fine. I don't need to go to parties to validate my social standing or anything like that.

jokes.text: I really would have to basically throw all my self-respect just right out the window. / Aww, that's so sweet.
jokes.setup: David dramatically explaining why he can't go to Mutt's party
jokes.punchline: Stevie's sarcastic response to his melodrama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie response - cutting through David's drama with dry sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I can't possibly go to Mutt's party. I have a previous engagement that simply cannot be rescheduled. It's a matter of utmost importance.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Let me guess, you're washing your hair?

jokes.text: Something more grand? / Ah, knowing your penchant for surprises, I'm guessing you have something else up your sleeve.
jokes.setup: Johnny giving Moira flowers for their anniversary
jokes.punchline: Moira's expectation of grander gestures revealing their different relationship dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Moira's expectations and Johnny's pressure to meet them

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Happy anniversary. I got you flowers.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Flowers? Johnny, these are the same flowers you get me every year.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You love flowers.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I love *gestures* — I love grand romantic gestures. I love being surprised. I love feeling like you've put thought into something.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I did put thought into it. I thought, 'Moira loves flowers.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, you thought wrong.

jokes.text: Sort of a 'Lady and the Tramp' meets '9 1/2 Weeks' kind of dinner date?
jokes.setup: Johnny describing their romantic dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Moira combining innocent Disney romance with explicit erotic thriller
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira moment - absurd cultural reference mixing that's perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, how romantic! A candlelit dinner. You know, it reminds me of that scene in Beauty and the Beast, except instead of Mrs. Potts pouring the tea, it's me pouring hot wax on your chest while you call me 'Mistress.'

jokes.text: You always could swing a good table at the last minute, John.
jokes.setup: Johnny claiming he made the reservation weeks ago after clearly calling that afternoon
jokes.punchline: Moira's pointed comment about his supposed skills at getting tables
jokes.explanation: Good marital dynamic humor showing Moira calling out Johnny's lie

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's quite impressive, considering you called them this afternoon.

jokes.text: Oh my God, the Roses! You're alive!
jokes.setup: Running into old wealthy friends at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Treating their survival after losing wealth as surprising news
jokes.explanation: Great line that reveals how their wealthy friends viewed their downfall

dialogue.speaker: Bev
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that the Hendersons? I heard they lost everything in the market crash. How are they even alive?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, they're right there.

dialogue.speaker: Bev
dialogue.line: I know, that's what I'm saying — it's remarkable.

jokes.text: I wouldn't do that if I were you... Mutt made those ice cubes from well water, so... they smell a little fishy.
jokes.setup: David about to put ice in his drink
jokes.punchline: Casual warning about disgusting ice cubes
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie moment with her casual delivery of gross information

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I wouldn't do that if I were you. Those ice cubes are from like three months ago.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? How is that possible?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: The ice maker's been broken since you got here. I've just been refreezing the same water.

jokes.text: Oh, I wouldn't either, but I couldn't find a cocktail shaker, and someone brought room temperature vodka.
jokes.setup: Jake warns David about the ice cubes
jokes.punchline: David explains his desperate mixology situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's prioritization of cocktails over health concerns.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm aware of the ice situation, thank you. When you're making cocktails with a blender that sounds like it's auditioning for a horror film, you take certain liberties with the ice cube situation.

jokes.text: It's nice to meet you, handsome.
jokes.setup: David introducing himself to Jake
jokes.punchline: Jake immediately flirting with David
jokes.explanation: Sets up the David/Jake dynamic that pays off later in the episode

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm David Rose.

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: Well hello there, David Rose. I'm Jake. And I have to say, you are absolutely stunning.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh. Thank you. I... that's very kind of you to say.

jokes.text: No, I've just never seen you this dressed up before. / I do what I want.
jokes.setup: David commenting on Stevie's appearance
jokes.punchline: Stevie's defensive but matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - economical and defensive without being emotional

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Your hair looks different today.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I didn't wash it. I'm not going to apologize for that.

jokes.text: Having swam in both ponds, I can say that I might be able to navigate those waters a little bit better than you.
jokes.setup: David claiming his 'stellar radar' for knowing when guys are into him
jokes.punchline: Stevie's aquatic metaphor for her bisexuality giving her better insight
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor that reveals character information while being funny

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, I can see both shores, so...

jokes.text: She's moved it to the front hall! / What?! / Grier wanted to hang her coat on the horns!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Izzy's stuffed bison
jokes.punchline: Someone treating the mounted animal as a coat rack
jokes.explanation: Establishes the wealthy friends' world but not particularly strong humor

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Is that my bison?

dialogue.speaker: Don
dialogue.line: Your bison? It's been here for years.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, it's a very distinctive bison.

dialogue.speaker: Bev
dialogue.line: It's just a coat rack now, Moira.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A coat rack? That's a mounted bison!

dialogue.speaker: Don
dialogue.line: It's holding coats.

jokes.text: Don and Borden are on their hands and knees, crawling through the doggie door in the kitchen!
jokes.setup: Story about party at Izzy's house
jokes.punchline: Grown wealthy men crawling through dog door
jokes.explanation: Visual absurdity but lacks the sharp character work of better moments

dialogue.speaker: Bev
dialogue.line: So these grown wealthy men are crawling through the dog door at Izzy's house.

jokes.text: Because before you were saying that you didn't wanna go out tonight, so... / No, no, we-we uh... / You can certainly join us, if you don't have your own table.
jokes.setup: Johnny caught in contradiction about wanting to be alone
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to recover while being condescending
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Johnny's social awkwardness when caught

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I need some time alone to think.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: But you just said you wanted to spend the afternoon with us.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, that was before I realized I needed to be alone. You see, when one reaches a certain level of sophistication, solitude becomes a necessity rather than a preference.

jokes.text: The way he describes you, it's like you're this... Beautiful little poem.
jokes.setup: Tennessee meeting Alexis for the first time
jokes.punchline: Overly effusive compliment that's trying too hard
jokes.explanation: Shows Tennessee's try-hard nature but not particularly sharp

dialogue.speaker: Tennessee
dialogue.line: Alexis, your hair is like a golden waterfall of sophistication cascading down your shoulders like a wealthy mermaid who won the lottery and decided to become a shampoo commercial.

jokes.text: Well, love 'little!'
jokes.setup: Being called a 'beautiful little poem'
jokes.punchline: Alexis focusing on and sarcastically appreciating the diminutive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis response - focusing on the wrong part of the compliment

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: A poem? I'm a poem. That's... that's sweet. A little poem. Not a big poem, just a little one. Like, a haiku-sized poem. A poem you'd find in a greeting card next to a picture of a cat in a tiny hat.

jokes.text: Like, a really pretty poem, or like, a mermaid, or something.
jokes.setup: Returning Tennessee's poetic compliment
jokes.punchline: Alexis's scattered attempt at a compliment with random comparisons
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis moment showing her superficial approach to compliments

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You're like a... a gazelle. But like, a smart gazelle. With better hair. And you smell like expensive cologne, which is good because some people smell like cheap cologne, which is just sad. But you don't. You smell like... confidence? Is that a smell? Anyway, you're great.

jokes.text: It was so nice to finally meet you, Tallahassee, so... / It's ah, Tennessee. / Yes, Tennessee. I love that name, it's so unique. Um, thank God you look the way you do, because that name on the wrong girl would just... Ooh!
jokes.setup: Alexis getting Tennessee's name wrong
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about how her looks save her from her name being ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Alexis moment - seemingly sweet but actually devastating, perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry, what was your name again?

dialogue.speaker: Tennessee
dialogue.line: Tennessee.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right, Tennessee. That's such a... unique name.

dialogue.speaker: Tennessee
dialogue.line: Thanks.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, it's really lucky you're as pretty as you are, because that name is absolutely ridiculous.

jokes.text: Oh, we're in a rustic, ranch style... Bungalow... that... It's very nice.
jokes.setup: Wealthy friends asking about their living situation
jokes.punchline: Moira's painful attempt to make the motel sound luxurious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's pride and attempt to maintain dignity

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, we're currently residing in a charming boutique establishment. It's very... intimate. The rooms are quite compact, which is very European. And the amenities are, shall we say, rustic in their authenticity. There's a wonderful homogeneity to the decor—everything matches because it's all the same, you see. The plumbing has character. Real character. You can hear it singing at all hours.

jokes.text: Very nice, it's got plenty of parking, and a TV in every room. / Oh, how many bedrooms? / Ten. Ten bedrooms!
jokes.setup: Johnny describing the motel amenities
jokes.punchline: Moira inflating the number of rooms to sound impressive
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the Rose's desperate attempt to sound successful

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We have 8 rooms available.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, when I describe it to people, I usually say we have closer to 12.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We have 8 rooms.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, but 12 sounds better.

jokes.text: That must be a nightmare to clean! / Um, Stevie does an adequate job. / Stevie's one of your staff? / Stevie's the only staff.
jokes.setup: Friends impressed by their supposed mansion
jokes.punchline: Revealing they have only one staff member who does an 'adequate' job
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the lie with Johnny's deflating honesty

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Of course, we have an extensive staff to maintain the property.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, quite a large operation.

dialogue.speaker: Bev
dialogue.line: How many people work for you?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, there's... one.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And he's adequate.

jokes.text: On our way here, we drove through a town... Not a word of a lie, called... 'Schittstown!' / Oh! No, it was called, 'Schittsville!' / And from the looks of it, the town was living up to its name!
jokes.setup: Friends telling story about passing through a town
jokes.punchline: Mocking the town where the Roses actually live, not knowing it
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony setup for Johnny's eventual explosion

dialogue.speaker: Don
dialogue.line: We drove through this town once. Worst place you've ever seen.

dialogue.speaker: Bev
dialogue.line: Absolute dump. Nothing there. Who would ever live there?

dialogue.speaker: Don
dialogue.line: I mean, what kind of person settles in a place like that?

jokes.text: What do you even call someone from Schittsville? A Schitter? A Schitthead?
jokes.setup: Making fun of the fictional town name
jokes.punchline: Crude wordplay about residents' names
jokes.explanation: Lazy pun humor that sets up Johnny's justified anger

jokes.text: And from the looks of it, the town was living up to its name!
jokes.setup: Don mentions the town name 'Schittsville'
jokes.punchline: Adds insult about the town's appearance
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation of the town-mocking. Functional cringe comedy but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Don
dialogue.line: Schittsville? What kind of name is Schittsville?

dialogue.speaker: Don
dialogue.line: It looks like someone sneezed out a town.

jokes.text: Cool? Alexis... I couldn't stop crying. I was scaring all the animals at work.
jokes.setup: Alexis commenting on how cool he seemed after their breakup
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was crying so much it frightened animals at the vet clinic
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that's both funny and genuinely touching

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You seemed so cool after we broke up.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I was crying so much it frightened the animals at the vet clinic.

jokes.text: When I had to go on our honeymoon without you, the zip line instructor at the Los Passions Couples Resort told me that I just gotta get through it.
jokes.setup: Ted explaining how he dealt with the breakup
jokes.punchline: Getting life advice from a zip line instructor at a couples resort he attended alone
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific detail that's perfectly Ted

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I went to this couples resort alone, and I ended up getting life advice from a zip line instructor.

jokes.text: Mmm! Hmm, I thought I was shame eating in private.
jokes.setup: Jake finding David eating alone
jokes.punchline: David's self-aware admission about his emotional eating
jokes.explanation: Good David moment showing his self-awareness and neuroses

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: David? What are you doing here alone?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm eating my feelings. Which, given my current emotional state, explains the portion size.

jokes.text: What is shame eating? / I don't think we have enough time for that tonight.
jokes.setup: Jake's innocent question about shame eating
jokes.punchline: David implying it would take all night to explain his issues
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response showing his neurotic self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: Do you shame eat?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Where would I even start?

jokes.text: Who do you have to kill around here to get another bottle of this undrinkable wine? / I think it's good. / I ordered the one with the highest alcohol content.
jokes.setup: Don complaining about the wine quality
jokes.punchline: Roland's simple, honest response about choosing wine by alcohol content
jokes.explanation: Good Roland moment showing his straightforward, practical nature

dialogue.speaker: Don
dialogue.line: This wine is absolutely undrinkable. The nose is all wrong, the finish is thin, and the body is completely absent.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I just pick the one with the highest alcohol content. Gets the job done.

jokes.text: Who do you have to kill around here to get another bottle of this undrinkable wine? I think it's good. I ordered the one with the highest alcohol content.
jokes.setup: Don complains about the wine quality
jokes.punchline: Roland defends it based purely on alcohol content
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Roland's practical approach vs Don's snobbery.

dialogue.speaker: Don
dialogue.line: This wine is absolutely ghastly. The nose is nonexistent, the palate is thin, and the finish is abrupt.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, it gets you drunk, doesn't it?

jokes.text: The joke is I'm sitting here in a half decent restaurant with my wife... and our friends... And all you two have done is complain about the food, and pretend that you didn't leave us high and dry after we lost everything.
jokes.setup: Don making jokes about Schittsville
jokes.punchline: Johnny's explosive honesty about their fair-weather friendship
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment where Johnny's dignity finally breaks through

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about our friendship, and I realize it's completely conditional. The moment things got difficult, you disappeared.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not fair. I was there for you.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Were you? Or were you just there when it was convenient? When it made you look good?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You're being ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Am I? Name one time you went out of your way for me when there was nothing in it for you.

jokes.text: Roland and Jocelyn here, could not have been more generous with what little they have. They found us a place to live, they've offered us their truck whenever we've needed it. They've invited us to their parties, they even offered to take us out to dinner tonight.
jokes.setup: Johnny defending Roland and Jocelyn
jokes.punchline: Contrasting their generosity with his wealthy friends' abandonment
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character growth moment showing Johnny's changed perspective

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You know, Roland and Jocelyn have been nothing but kind to us since we arrived in this town. When our friends from the city heard about our situation, they scattered like rats. But these people—they've opened their homes, their hearts. I won't have them disrespected.

jokes.text: Well, just to be clear, Johnny, we were always gonna split the bill, it's just with the coupon...
jokes.setup: Johnny praising Roland's generosity
jokes.punchline: Roland's oblivious clarification about the bill splitting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland moment - completely missing the emotional moment to clarify practical details

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, that was very generous of you to pick up the bill.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, I didn't pick up the bill. I just didn't pay my half.

jokes.text: And that town you passed through, it's not called Schittsville. It's called Schitt's Creek. And it's where we live.
jokes.setup: Friends mocking the fictional Schittsville
jokes.punchline: Johnny revealing they actually live in that town
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup, with dignity and dramatic impact

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, we actually live here now.

jokes.text: Christie McFadden accidentally nailed her hand to her paper towel holder, so that's-the lesson is just not to nail stuff...
jokes.setup: David claiming he did woodworking in high school
jokes.punchline: Revealing his 'experience' was witnessing a horrific accident
jokes.explanation: Good David moment showing his tendency to overshare inappropriate details

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I did woodworking in high school.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I witnessed a horrific accident in woodworking class.

jokes.text: I'm a pretty open-minded guy. So... why don't you stop by the shop sometime?
jokes.setup: David's awkward flirting and oversharing
jokes.punchline: Jake's direct invitation despite David's weirdness
jokes.explanation: Sets up the competition between David and Stevie

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I just think it's important to establish a baseline of emotional availability before we engage in any sort of physical intimacy. I have a very specific skincare routine that takes about forty-five minutes, and I need to know if you're comfortable with that level of commitment.

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: Do you want to get dinner?

jokes.text: Some townies were trying to get us high, and we didn't want to offend them, because they're doing so well in life.
jokes.setup: David asking where she was
jokes.punchline: Alexis's backwards logic about not wanting to offend drug dealers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis logic - completely backwards priorities delivered with total sincerity

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Where were you?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I was at this really sketchy part of town, and I didn't want to offend any of the drug dealers, so I just smiled and waved at everyone.

jokes.text: Because we just necked out behind the barn. / Seriously, what makes you think that? / Because we put our mouths together, out behind the barn.
jokes.setup: Stevie questioning David's claim about Jake being into him
jokes.punchline: David's increasingly specific and crude descriptions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing David's desperation to prove himself

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Wait, Jake is into you?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, he looked at me a certain way.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: What way?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A way that suggested he wanted to do things to me. Specific things. Crude things.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Did he say any words?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, but his eyes were very loud.

jokes.text: Just so you know, I kissed him too. / Well, that didn't happen. / Who's to say?
jokes.setup: David bragging about kissing Jake
jokes.punchline: Stevie's casual revelation and David's denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie moment - casual devastation of David's ego

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I kissed Jake Champ.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I know. He told me.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? No, he didn't.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: He did. He said it was nice.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, he's lying.

jokes.text: Did he invite you to his wood shop? / Sorry, that came out wrong.
jokes.setup: David trying to prove Jake likes him more
jokes.punchline: Accidental sexual innuendo in his question
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo joke that fits David's neurotic character

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Jake likes me more than you. I can prove it.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: How?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'll ask him who he'd rather have go down on him.

jokes.text: Okay, this is not okay! What are you guys doing here?! / Shhh, Alexis! For once in your life Shhhh!
jokes.setup: Alexis finding her parents high at Mutt's party
jokes.punchline: Johnny's desperate attempt to quiet her down
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the parents' night getting out of hand

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, are you guys high right now?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, no, no. Alexis, keep your voice down.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You're high! You're both high!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We are not high. We just... we had some of Mutt's special brownies, and we may have miscalculated the dosage.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Alexis, please. Your mother is having a very important conversation with a squirrel right now, and I would appreciate it if you didn't draw attention to us.

jokes.text: I don't care what snarky remarks you kids are gonna make under your breath, tonight we are dancing as a family.
jokes.setup: High Johnny declaring family bonding time
jokes.punchline: His drug-induced earnestness about forced family dancing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's drug-induced vulnerability

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We're going to have a family bonding experience. We're going to dance together.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, no.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes. Come on, get up. Let's dance as a family.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm not dancing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You are all going to dance with me right now, and you're going to like it.

jokes.text: Do you know your father and I hold great affection for the two of you? It may not always be obvious, but... Just say it, Moira! We love you both very much.
jokes.setup: Moira's typically formal way of expressing emotion
jokes.punchline: Johnny forcing her to just say 'I love you' directly
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment showing both parents' struggles with expressing emotion

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You are the most extraordinary man. Your presence in my life has fundamentally altered the trajectory of my existence in ways that defy articulation.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, just say 'I love you.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I love you.

tmdb_overview: Johnny and Moira go out for their anniversary and run into a couple they knew from their former life, while Alexis and David attend Mutt’s annual barn party, making up with some friends, and making out with others.
insight: Season finale leans on character cringe over punchlines, hitting just 3 jokes per minute.
